g.data-narration: Arrays in Java are fundamental data structures that store multiple values of the same type in a single variable, enabling efficient data management.
An array is a fixed-size indexed data structure that stores multiple values of the same type in contiguous memory locations.
g.text: A collection of elements of the same type stored in
contiguous memory locations, accessed using an index.

g.data-narration: Instead of declaring multiple separate variables, arrays let you store related data in one structure, enabling efficient random access to any element.
Java arrays can be declared in three ways: using new keyword with size, using initializer syntax, or C-style declaration.
Array indices in Java start from 0 and range to length-1. Accessing invalid indices throws ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
When arrays are created with new keyword, primitive numeric arrays initialize to 0, boolean to false, and reference types to null.
g.text: When creating arrays with new keyword:

g.data-narration: Multi-dimensional arrays in Java are arrays of arrays. A 2D array represents a table with rows and columns, stored as arrays of arrays.
Jagged arrays are 2D arrays where each row can have a different number of columns, providing flexibility in memory allocation.
g.text: Each row can have different column lengths:

g.data-narration: Arrays can be traversed using traditional for loops or enhanced for-each loops. Each has specific use cases and limitations.
g.text: ⚠ Enhanced for cannot modify elements or access indices

g.data-narration: The Arrays class (java.util.Arrays) provides static methods for sorting, searching, filling, and converting arrays to strings.
Finding maximum and minimum requires initializing with first element, then comparing each subsequent element to update max/min values.
Matrix addition involves adding corresponding elements from two matrices of the same dimensions using nested loops.
Avoid these common mistakes: using wrong loop bounds, confusing .length property with .length() method, and trying to change array size after creation.
g.text: Arrays cannot resize - create new one

g.data-narration: In summary, arrays are fixed-size indexed data structures in Java with zero-based indexing, supporting single and multi-dimensional forms.

g.text: ✓ Arrays store multiple values of same type in contiguous memory
✓ Zero-based indexing: valid range is 0 to length-1
✓ Use .length property (not .length() method) for array size
✓ Arrays utility class: sort(), binarySearch(), fill(), toString()
✓ 2D arrays in Java are arrays of arrays (supports jagged arrays)
✓ Remember: Arrays have fixed size - cannot change after creation

g.data-narration: For exams: remember default values, preferred declaration syntax, and practice array operations. ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException is commonly tested.
g.text: ✓ int[] arr preferred over int arr[]
